interfaces.name: TableProcessor
interfaces.signalFlowRole: Lookup table step in signal path - used by Curve and AsymetricalCurve shaping modes

parameterGroups.label: Pre-Shaper Filtering
parameterGroups.rationale: Filters applied to the wet signal before the shaping function, plus toggle to bypass them

parameters.name: HighPass
parameters.description: High-pass cutoff frequency before the shaper

parameters.name: LowPass
parameters.description: Low-pass cutoff frequency before the shaper

parameters.name: BypassFilters
parameters.description: Bypasses the pre-shaper high-pass and low-pass filters

parameterGroups.label: Input Stage
parameterGroups.rationale: Controls applied to the signal before the shaping function: gain, bias, and soft limiting

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: Input gain in decibels applied before shaping

parameters.name: BiasLeft
parameters.description: DC bias applied to the left channel before shaping

parameters.name: BiasRight
parameters.description: DC bias applied to the right channel before shaping

parameters.name: LimitInput
parameters.description: Enables a soft limiter before the input to avoid hard clipping

parameterGroups.label: Shaping
parameterGroups.rationale: Core shaping parameters: mode selection, drive, and oversampling quality

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: Selects the shaping function used to process the signal

parameters.name: Drive
parameters.description: Drive amount applied to the shaper input

parameters.name: Oversampling
parameters.description: Oversampling factor to reduce aliasing in nonlinear shaping

parameters.name: Reduce
parameters.description: Bit reduction amount in bits for lo-fi distortion

parameterGroups.label: Output Stage
parameterGroups.rationale: Controls applied after shaping: autogain compensation and dry/wet mix

parameters.name: Autogain
parameters.description: Automatically compensates output level based on the shaping curve

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.description: Dry and wet balance where 0.0 is fully dry and 1.0 is fully wet

gaps.question: What is the exact processing order in applyEffect()? Specifically: where do filters, gain, bias, limiter, shaping, bitcrushing, DC removal, autogain, and mix sit relative to each other?
gaps.context: Need to author accurate pseudo-code showing the full signal chain from input to output.

gaps.question: The Drive parameter (0-1 normalised) is stored but how and where is it applied in applyEffect()? Is it redundant with Gain?
gaps.context: Drive and Gain both seem to control input level - need to clarify their distinct roles for the parameter table.

gaps.question: What shaping functions are available for each Mode value (0-33)? Which modes use the TableProcessor lookup table?
gaps.context: Users need to know what each mode does. The table-based modes (Curve, AsymetricalCurve) are the TableProcessor interface usage.

gaps.question: Where does the TableProcessor lookup occur in the signal path? Is it the shaping function itself for Curve mode?
gaps.context: Required by the preliminary gate checklist since TableProcessor is listed as an interface.

gaps.question: Does oversampling introduce latency? How is the dry buffer compensated for the latency? What are the CPU implications of each oversampling factor?
gaps.context: Users need to understand the performance/quality trade-off of oversampling settings.

gaps.question: How is the autogain value calculated? Is it derived from the shaping curve at configuration time, or dynamically?
gaps.context: Autogain behaviour affects how users should set gain staging.

gaps.question: Is there DC removal after the shaper? The bias parameters add DC offset - is it removed post-shaping?
gaps.context: Users adding bias need to know whether DC offset persists in the output.

gaps.question: How is the dry/wet mix implemented? Is the dry copy taken before or after filtering?
gaps.context: Affects whether the dry signal includes the pre-shaper filtering or is the unfiltered original.

notes: ShapeFX has no modulation chains - it is a purely parameter-driven effect. The Mode parameter selects from multiple waveshaping functions including mathematical functions (atan, tanh, sin etc.) and table-based curves. The Drive parameter's role vs Gain is unclear from base data alone. The TableProcessor interface is used for the Curve shaping mode. BypassFilters gates the HighPass/LowPass parameters. The module also implements WaveformComponent::Broadcaster for UI display but this is not a signal-path interface.
